migrationSteps: Replace import: com.vaadin.ui.TextField → com.vaadin.flow.component.textfield.TextField
Remove deprecated setValue/getValue and use standard Flow API
Verify label placement (Flow uses label as constructor arg differently)
Replace import: com.vaadin.ui.TextArea → com.vaadin.flow.component.textfield.TextArea
Update rows/cols configuration using setMinRows/setMaxRows
Verify resize handling (Flow uses CSS resize instead of API)
Replace import: com.vaadin.ui.PasswordField → com.vaadin.flow.component.textfield.PasswordField
Verify reveal button configuration if any custom reveal logic exists
Replace import: com.vaadin.ui.Button → com.vaadin.flow.component.button.Button
Replace addClickListener lambda (API is identical)
Replace setIcon() with Vaadin Flow icon API (VaadinIcon.create())
Replace import: com.vaadin.ui.Label → com.vaadin.flow.component.html.Span
Replace setValue(text) with setText(text) or use Div for block-level display
If ContentMode.HTML was used, use Div with getElement().setProperty('innerHTML', html)
Replace import: com.vaadin.ui.CheckBox → com.vaadin.flow.component.checkbox.Checkbox
Note: Flow class is Checkbox (lowercase 'b') vs Vaadin 7 CheckBox (uppercase 'B')
Replace import: com.vaadin.ui.ComboBox → com.vaadin.flow.component.combobox.ComboBox
Replace addItem/setContainerDataSource with setItems(List) or setItemLabelGenerator
Verify filtering: Flow ComboBox uses setItems(query → stream) for lazy loading
Replace import: com.vaadin.ui.DateField → com.vaadin.flow.component.datepicker.DatePicker
Update value type: Flow DatePicker uses LocalDate not java.util.Date
Replace format string with DatePickerI18n if locale formatting was customized
Replace import: com.vaadin.ui.Image → com.vaadin.flow.component.html.Image
Replace setSource(Resource) with setSrc(String url) or StreamResource
Remove Vaadin 7 ThemeResource/ExternalResource usage
Replace import: com.vaadin.ui.Link → com.vaadin.flow.component.html.Anchor
Replace setResource/setCaption with setHref/setText
For router links use RouterLink instead of Anchor
Replace import: com.vaadin.ui.MenuBar → com.vaadin.flow.component.menubar.MenuBar
Replace addItem(caption, command) with addItem(text, e -> ...) lambda
Verify submenu nesting: Flow uses addSubMenu().addItem()
Replace import: com.vaadin.ui.ProgressBar → com.vaadin.flow.component.progressbar.ProgressBar
Verify setIndeterminate(true/false) — same API in Flow
Replace import: com.vaadin.ui.Upload → com.vaadin.flow.component.upload.Upload
Replace Receiver/SucceededListener with SucceededEvent lambda
Use MemoryBuffer or MultiFileMemoryBuffer for in-memory uploads
Replace import: com.vaadin.ui.Notification → com.vaadin.flow.component.notification.Notification
Replace Notification.show(msg) with Notification.show(msg) — same static API
Replace delay via setDuration(ms) instead of Vaadin 7 int delay arg
Replace import: com.vaadin.ui.VerticalLayout → com.vaadin.flow.component.orderedlayout.VerticalLayout
Replace addComponent(c) with add(c)
Verify spacing/margin: Flow uses setSpacing/setPadding booleans
Replace import: com.vaadin.ui.HorizontalLayout → com.vaadin.flow.component.orderedlayout.HorizontalLayout
Replace addComponent(c) with add(c)
Replace setExpandRatio(c, ratio) with setFlexGrow(ratio, c)
Replace import: com.vaadin.ui.FormLayout → com.vaadin.flow.component.formlayout.FormLayout
Replace addComponent(c) with add(c)
Columns now defined via setResponsiveSteps() instead of setColumns()
Replace import: com.vaadin.ui.CssLayout → com.vaadin.flow.component.html.Div
Replace addComponent(c) with add(c)
CSS class styling remains the same via addClassName()

rules.context: Styling and layout properties need manual adjustment after type rename
migrationSteps: Replace import: com.vaadin.ui.Panel → com.vaadin.flow.component.html.Div
Add CSS border/padding styling to replicate Panel visual appearance
If caption was used, add a header element manually
Review scrolling behavior — Panel had built-in scroll; Div needs overflow CSS
Replace import: com.vaadin.ui.NativeSelect → com.vaadin.flow.component.select.Select
Replace addItem/setContainerDataSource with setItems(List)
Verify value change listener — Flow uses addValueChangeListener
Replace import: com.vaadin.ui.ListSelect → com.vaadin.flow.component.listbox.ListBox
Replace addItem/setContainerDataSource with setItems(List)
For multi-select use MultiSelectListBox instead

rules.context: OptionGroup supported both single and multi-select; must determine which mode was used
migrationSteps: Determine if OptionGroup was used in single-select or multi-select mode
Single-select: replace with RadioButtonGroup from com.vaadin.flow.component.radiobutton
Multi-select: replace with CheckboxGroup from com.vaadin.flow.component.checkbox
Replace addItem/setContainerDataSource with setItems(List)

rules.context: No direct Slider equivalent in Vaadin 24 core; use NumberField or web component
migrationSteps: Replace with NumberField (com.vaadin.flow.component.textfield.NumberField) for simple numeric input
Or use vcf-slider web component from Vaadin Directory for slider UI
Set min/max/step constraints on NumberField: setMin(), setMax(), setStep()
Replace import: com.vaadin.ui.RichTextArea → com.vaadin.flow.component.richtexteditor.RichTextEditor
Add dependency: vaadin-rich-text-editor (from Vaadin Pro components)
Replace getValue/setValue with the Flow RichTextEditor API

rules.context: No direct twin-column UI in Vaadin 24 core; use MultiSelectListBox or a custom layout
migrationSteps: Replace with MultiSelectListBox for functional equivalence
Or create a two-column layout with two ListBoxes and transfer buttons
Replace addItem/setContainerDataSource with setItems(List)

rules.context: Inline mode not directly available; use DatePicker with setOpened(true) or a calendar web component
migrationSteps: Replace with DatePicker (com.vaadin.flow.component.datepicker.DatePicker)
Call setOpened(true) to show picker inline if always-open behavior is required
Update value type from java.util.Date to java.time.LocalDate
Replace import: com.vaadin.ui.PopupDateField → com.vaadin.flow.component.datepicker.DatePicker
DatePicker is popup by default — no additional configuration needed
Update value type from java.util.Date to java.time.LocalDate
Replace import: com.vaadin.ui.ColorPicker → com.vaadin.flow.component.colorpicker.ColorPicker
Add dependency: vaadin-color-picker (Vaadin Pro component)
Replace Color object usage with String hex values in Flow
Replace import: com.vaadin.ui.Audio → com.vaadin.flow.component.html.Audio
Replace setSource(Resource) with setSrc(String url)
Verify autoplay/controls attribute handling
Replace import: com.vaadin.ui.Video → com.vaadin.flow.component.html.Video
Replace setSource(Resource) with setSrc(String url)
Verify poster/controls attribute handling

rules.context: Embedded served multiple purposes; determine whether it embedded a URL (use IFrame) or Flash/image
migrationSteps: If source was an ExternalResource URL: replace with IFrame (com.vaadin.flow.component.html.IFrame)
If source was an image: replace with Image (com.vaadin.flow.component.html.Image)
If source was Flash: Flash is no longer supported; remove Flash content

rules.context: Flash is no longer supported in any browser. Flash content must be replaced with HTML5 equivalents.
migrationSteps: Remove Flash component entirely
Replace Flash video/animation with HTML5 video/canvas or a JS library
Identify what the Flash content was doing and find a modern HTML5 equivalent
Replace import: com.vaadin.ui.BrowserFrame → com.vaadin.flow.component.html.IFrame
Replace setSource(Resource) with setSrc(String url)
Replace import: com.vaadin.ui.NativeButton → com.vaadin.flow.component.button.Button
Add addThemeVariants(ButtonVariant.LUMO_TERTIARY) if minimal styling is needed
Replace addClickListener lambda — same API in Flow

rules.context: AbsoluteLayout has no equivalent in Vaadin 24. Absolute positioning must be implemented via CSS.
migrationSteps: Replace AbsoluteLayout with a Div (com.vaadin.flow.component.html.Div)
Apply CSS position: relative to the Div container
For each child, apply position: absolute with top/left coordinates via getStyle()
Review whether absolute positioning is still needed or can be replaced with Flexbox

rules.context: GridLayout has no direct equivalent in Vaadin 24. CSS Grid or FormLayout must be used.
migrationSteps: Identify grid dimensions (rows/columns) from GridLayout configuration
Replace with FormLayout for form-style grids or a Div with CSS grid styling
Apply grid-template-columns/rows via getStyle().set('display','grid')
Re-implement span/align logic using grid-column-start/end CSS properties

rules.context: SplitPanel API changed significantly; component placement and split position API differ
migrationSteps: Replace import: com.vaadin.ui.SplitPanel → com.vaadin.flow.component.splitlayout.SplitLayout
Replace setFirstComponent/setSecondComponent with addToPrimary/addToSecondary
Replace setSplitPosition(percent) with setSplitterPosition(percent)

rules.context: Use SplitLayout with HORIZONTAL orientation
migrationSteps: Replace import: com.vaadin.ui.HorizontalSplitPanel → com.vaadin.flow.component.splitlayout.SplitLayout
Set orientation: new SplitLayout(SplitLayout.Orientation.HORIZONTAL)
Replace setFirstComponent/setSecondComponent with addToPrimary/addToSecondary

rules.context: Use SplitLayout with VERTICAL orientation
migrationSteps: Replace import: com.vaadin.ui.VerticalSplitPanel → com.vaadin.flow.component.splitlayout.SplitLayout
Set orientation: new SplitLayout(SplitLayout.Orientation.VERTICAL)
Replace setFirstComponent/setSecondComponent with addToPrimary/addToSecondary
Replace import: com.vaadin.ui.Accordion → com.vaadin.flow.component.accordion.Accordion
Replace addTab(caption, content) with add(AccordionPanel) or add(caption, content)
Verify open/close listener API changes

rules.context: No direct PopupView equivalent in Vaadin 24; use Dialog or ContextMenu
migrationSteps: Replace with Dialog (com.vaadin.flow.component.dialog.Dialog) for modal popups
Replace with ContextMenu (com.vaadin.flow.component.contextmenu.ContextMenu) for contextual popups
Implement popup trigger logic manually using Button click → dialog.open()

rules.context: Vaadin 24 has a new drag-and-drop API (DragSource/DropTarget) that is structurally different
migrationSteps: Remove DragAndDropWrapper usage
Implement drag source using DragSource.create(component)
Implement drop target using DropTarget.create(component)
Replace DragAndDropEvent handlers with DragStartEvent/DropEvent listeners
Verify browser drag-and-drop data transfer if file drops were involved

rules.context: Vaadin Calendar is not included in Vaadin 24 core; a third-party calendar component is needed
migrationSteps: Identify which calendar features were used (events, scheduling, date navigation)
Evaluate Full Calendar for Vaadin add-on (vcalendar)
Or use a plain DatePicker/DateTimePicker for simple date selection use cases
Migrate event data model to the new component's data API

rules.context: Table has no direct equivalent in Vaadin 24. Migration requires replacing with Grid, adding column definitions, and migrating container data sources to DataProvider.
migrationSteps: Replace com.vaadin.ui.Table with com.vaadin.flow.component.grid.Grid<T>
Define columns: grid.addColumn(T::getField).setHeader('Header')
Replace Container/BeanItemContainer data source with setItems(list) or DataProvider
Migrate row click listeners: grid.addItemClickListener(e -> ...)
Review column generators: use ComponentRenderer for custom cell content

rules.context: Vaadin 7 Grid API differs significantly from Vaadin 24 Grid; column and data binding patterns changed
migrationSteps: Replace import: com.vaadin.ui.Grid → com.vaadin.flow.component.grid.Grid
Replace addColumn(propertyId) with addColumn(T::getMethod).setHeader('...')
Replace setContainerDataSource with setItems(list) or DataProvider
Migrate SelectionMode and selection listeners to new API
Review editor (inline editing) configuration if used

rules.context: Window is replaced by Dialog in Vaadin 24. Layout and positioning API has changed.
migrationSteps: Replace com.vaadin.ui.Window with com.vaadin.flow.component.dialog.Dialog
Replace setContent(component) by adding components via dialog.add(component)
Replace open()/close() with setOpened(true/false) or open()/close()
Draggable behavior: call dialog.setDraggable(true) explicitly
Replace WindowMoveListener with MoveEvent if window position tracking is needed

rules.context: TabSheet is replaced by Tabs component in Vaadin 24. Tab content wiring pattern changed.
migrationSteps: Replace com.vaadin.ui.TabSheet with com.vaadin.flow.component.tabs.Tabs
Create Tab objects: Tab tab = new Tab('Caption')
Wire tab-to-content: maintain a Map<Tab, Component> and show/hide on selection change
Listen to selection: tabs.addSelectedChangeListener(e -> showTab(e.getSelectedTab()))
Place Tabs and content Div side by side in a VerticalLayout

rules.context: Tree/TreeTable are replaced by TreeGrid in Vaadin 24. Hierarchical data model must be migrated to TreeDataProvider or HierarchicalDataProvider.
migrationSteps: Replace com.vaadin.ui.Tree with com.vaadin.flow.component.treegrid.TreeGrid<T>
Create TreeDataProvider<T> with getChildren() lambda or implement HierarchicalDataProvider
Replace HierarchicalContainer with TreeData<T> and TreeDataProvider<T>
Replace Item/Property API with bean property access
Add expand/collapse listeners if needed

rules.context: Tree/TreeTable are replaced by TreeGrid in Vaadin 24. Hierarchical data model must be migrated to TreeDataProvider or HierarchicalDataProvider.
migrationSteps: Replace com.vaadin.ui.TreeTable with com.vaadin.flow.component.treegrid.TreeGrid<T>
Add columns: grid.addHierarchyColumn(T::getName).setHeader('Name')
Create TreeDataProvider<T> with getChildren() lambda
Migrate all column generators to ColumnRenderers
Replace ContainerHierarchicalWrapper with TreeData<T>

rules.context: CustomComponent is replaced by Composite in Vaadin 24. Component composition pattern may need structural changes.
migrationSteps: Replace extends CustomComponent with extends Composite<T> where T is the root component type
Replace setCompositionRoot(component) with overriding initContent() returning the root component
Remove Vaadin 7 constructor pattern and adopt Flow lifecycle
Verify that component state is managed correctly with the new composition model

rules.context: UI class extension is replaced by @Route + AppLayout pattern in Vaadin 24. Application structure must be redesigned.
migrationSteps: Remove extends UI — there is no UI subclassing in Vaadin 24
Create a MainLayout class extending AppLayout for shell/navigation
Replace init(VaadinRequest) with @Route views and @PreserveOnRefresh if needed
Move global error handling to VaadinServiceInitListener
Register route targets with @Route(value='path', layout=MainLayout.class)

rules.context: BeanItemContainer is replaced by DataProvider in Vaadin 24. Requires structural changes to data loading and filtering patterns.
migrationSteps: Replace BeanItemContainer<T> with DataProvider<T, Void> or ListDataProvider<T>
Use DataProvider.ofCollection(list) for simple in-memory lists
Implement CallbackDataProvider for lazy/backend-driven data loading
Replace addBean/addAll with DataProvider backed by the underlying collection
Replace Container.Filter with DataProvider filter predicates

rules.context: FieldGroup/BeanFieldGroup is replaced by Binder in Vaadin 24. Binding declarations and commit/discard patterns must be rewritten.
migrationSteps: Replace BeanFieldGroup<T> with Binder<T>
Replace buildAndBind(propertyId) with binder.forField(field).bind(T::getX, T::setX)
Replace commit() with binder.writeBeanIfValid(bean) or binder.writeBean(bean)
Replace discard() with binder.readBean(bean)
Add validation: binder.forField(field).withValidator(...).bind(...)

rules.context: FieldGroup/BeanFieldGroup is replaced by Binder in Vaadin 24. Binding declarations and commit/discard patterns must be rewritten.
migrationSteps: Replace FieldGroup with Binder<T>
Replace bind(field, propertyId) with binder.forField(field).bind(T::getX, T::setX)
Replace commit() with binder.writeBeanIfValid(bean)
Replace discard() with binder.readBean(bean)
Migrate FieldGroup.CommitException handling to BinderValidationStatus

rules.context: The Vaadin 7 Property interface is removed in Vaadin 24; use direct field binding via Binder or ValueProvider
migrationSteps: Remove all com.vaadin.data.Property usages
Replace Property<T> value sources with Binder bindings or ValueProvider lambdas
Replace ObjectProperty with simple fields or JavaFX-style observable properties
Replace ValueChangeListener with Flow addValueChangeListener events

rules.context: The Vaadin 7 Item interface is removed in Vaadin 24; use bean objects directly with Binder or DataProvider
migrationSteps: Remove all com.vaadin.data.Item usages
Replace Item with a plain POJO/bean and use Binder for field binding
Replace getItemProperty(id) with direct bean getter methods
If Item was used as table row, use typed Grid<T> with bean objects

rules.context: The Vaadin 7 Container interface is removed in Vaadin 24; use DataProvider instead
migrationSteps: Remove all com.vaadin.data.Container usages
Replace Container with DataProvider<T, F> or ListDataProvider<T>
Replace Container.addItem / getItem with DataProvider backed collections
Replace Container.Filter with Predicate or DataProvider filter functions

rules.context: Vaadin 7 Validator interface differs from Flow Validator<T>; validation is now part of Binder
migrationSteps: Replace com.vaadin.data.Validator with com.vaadin.flow.data.binder.Validator<T>
Rewrite validate() to return ValidationResult.ok() or ValidationResult.error(msg)
Wire validators via binder.forField(field).withValidator(validator).bind(...)
Replace InvalidValueException with returning ValidationResult.error(message)

rules.context: Converter API changed from Vaadin 7 to Flow; method signatures differ
migrationSteps: Replace import: com.vaadin.data.util.converter.Converter → com.vaadin.flow.data.converter.Converter
Rewrite convertToModel(value, locale) as convertToModel(value, context)
Rewrite convertToPresentation(value, locale) as convertToPresentation(value, context)
Replace ConversionException with returning Result.error(message)

rules.context: IndexedContainer is removed in Vaadin 24; replace with typed DataProvider or ListDataProvider
migrationSteps: Identify the data type stored in IndexedContainer
Create a typed List<T> and wrap with DataProvider.ofCollection(list)
Replace addContainerProperty / getItem / addItem with bean-based operations
Wire to Grid or other components via typed DataProvider

rules.context: HierarchicalContainer is removed in Vaadin 24; use TreeDataProvider for hierarchical data
migrationSteps: Replace HierarchicalContainer with TreeData<T> and TreeDataProvider<T>
Implement T::getChildren as the child supplier for TreeDataProvider
Wire to TreeGrid<T> instead of Tree or TreeTable

rules.context: Container filters are removed in Vaadin 24; filtering is done at DataProvider level
migrationSteps: Replace SimpleStringFilter with a Predicate<T> passed to ListDataProvider.setFilter()
Or implement filtering in a CallbackDataProvider query handler
Remove all Container.Filter and Container.Filterable usage

rules.context: View interface is replaced by @Route annotation in Vaadin 24. Navigator lifecycle methods (enter/beforeLeave) must be replaced with BeforeEnterObserver/etc.
migrationSteps: Remove implements View from the class
Add @Route(value='path') annotation to the class
Replace enter(ViewChangeListener.ViewChangeEvent event) with beforeEnter(BeforeEnterEvent event) from BeforeEnterObserver
Replace beforeLeave() with BeforeLeaveObserver.beforeLeave(BeforeLeaveEvent)
Remove Navigator references and use UI.navigate(Class) or RouteParameters instead

rules.context: Navigator is removed in Vaadin 24; the Flow router handles navigation declaratively via @Route
migrationSteps: Remove Navigator instantiation and configuration
Replace navigator.navigateTo('path') with UI.getCurrent().navigate('path') or navigate(ViewClass.class)
Replace addView(name, viewClass) with @Route annotation on the view class
Remove ViewProvider implementations — use @RouteAlias for aliases

rules.context: ViewChangeListener is removed; use BeforeEnterObserver / AfterNavigationObserver in Vaadin 24
migrationSteps: Remove ViewChangeListener implementations
For navigation-in: implement BeforeEnterObserver on the view class
For navigation-out: implement BeforeLeaveObserver on the view class
For post-navigation: implement AfterNavigationObserver

rules.context: ViewDisplay is removed in Vaadin 24; the router handles view rendering automatically
migrationSteps: Remove ViewDisplay implementations
Use RouterLayout interface if a shell layout is needed
The router automatically renders @Route views into the layout slot

rules.context: ViewProvider is removed in Vaadin 24; use dynamic route parameters or RouteAlias
migrationSteps: Remove ViewProvider implementations
For parameterized views: implement HasUrlParameter<T> on the view or use @RouteParameters
For aliases: use @RouteAlias annotation
For dynamic routing: implement DynamicRoute (advanced, rarely needed)

rules.context: VaadinServlet is no longer extended; Vaadin 24 auto-configures via Spring Boot integration
migrationSteps: Remove extends VaadinServlet — no Servlet extension needed in Spring Boot
Remove web.xml or @WebServlet configuration
Spring Boot auto-configuration registers the Vaadin servlet automatically
Move any custom servlet logic to VaadinServiceInitListener
Replace import: com.vaadin.server.VaadinService → com.vaadin.flow.server.VaadinService
Review API changes: some Vaadin 7 methods may have been removed or renamed
Verify getCurrent() usage — same pattern in Flow
Replace import: com.vaadin.server.VaadinSession → com.vaadin.flow.server.VaadinSession
getCurrent() and setAttribute/getAttribute API is the same
Review lock/unlock patterns if manual session locking was used
Replace import: com.vaadin.server.VaadinRequest → com.vaadin.flow.server.VaadinRequest
Review API: getParameter, getHeader, getPathInfo — same in Flow
Replace VaadinServletRequest with VaadinRequest.getCurrent() if needed
Replace import: com.vaadin.server.VaadinResponse → com.vaadin.flow.server.VaadinResponse
Review API for header/cookie manipulation — generally same in Flow
Replace import: com.vaadin.server.Page → com.vaadin.flow.component.page.Page
Replace Page.getCurrent() with UI.getCurrent().getPage()
Replace open(url, windowName) with UI.getCurrent().getPage().open(url)
Replace setTitle(title) with UI.getCurrent().getPage().setTitle(title)

rules.context: ClientConnector is an internal Vaadin 7 extension point; Vaadin 24 uses web components and Element API
migrationSteps: Remove ClientConnector extension
Use Element API (getElement()) for low-level component interaction
Use custom web components for advanced UI extension scenarios
Review if the connector logic can be replaced by a plain Flow component

rules.context: Resource interface replaced by String URLs or StreamResource in Vaadin 24
migrationSteps: Replace ExternalResource with a String URL passed directly to component setters
Replace ClassResource/ThemeResource with URL string from StaticFileHandler or @Theme
Replace StreamResource with com.vaadin.flow.server.StreamResource (same concept, updated API)
Replace ExternalResource with a plain String URL
Pass the URL string directly to component.setSrc() or component.setHref()

rules.context: FileResource replaced by StreamResource in Vaadin 24; requires InputStream factory
migrationSteps: Replace FileResource with StreamResource
Provide InputStreamFactory: new StreamResource(filename, () -> new FileInputStream(file))
Register with UI.getCurrent().getSession().getResourceRegistry() if needed

rules.context: ThemeResource replaced by Lumo CSS custom properties or @Theme annotation in Vaadin 24
migrationSteps: Replace ThemeResource image paths with static resource URLs (frontend/ folder)
Use @CssImport or @Theme annotation for theme-level resources
Move images to src/main/frontend/images/ and reference via relative URL

rules.context: StreamResource API changed from Vaadin 7 to Flow
migrationSteps: Replace import: com.vaadin.server.StreamResource → com.vaadin.flow.server.StreamResource
Change constructor: new StreamResource(filename, inputStreamSource) where source is InputStreamFactory
Replace StreamSource.getStream() with InputStreamFactory.createInputStream()
Replace import: com.vaadin.server.ErrorHandler → com.vaadin.flow.server.ErrorHandler
Replace error(ErrorEvent event) with error(ErrorEvent event) — same method signature
Wire via VaadinSession.getCurrent().setErrorHandler(handler)
Replace import: com.vaadin.server.SessionDestroyListener → com.vaadin.flow.server.SessionDestroyListener
Register via VaadinService.addSessionDestroyListener() — same pattern
Replace import: com.vaadin.server.RequestHandler → com.vaadin.flow.server.RequestHandler
handleRequest(VaadinSession, VaadinRequest, VaadinResponse) signature is identical
Register via VaadinService.addRequestHandler() or VaadinServiceInitListener

rules.context: ItemClickEvent is removed; use Grid.addItemClickListener returning ItemClickEvent<T>
migrationSteps: Remove com.vaadin.event.ItemClickEvent import
Replace table.addItemClickListener with grid.addItemClickListener(e -> ...)
Access clicked item via e.getItem() returning typed T
Column clicked: e.getColumn() is a Grid.Column<T>

rules.context: LayoutEvents click handling removed; use component-level click events in Vaadin 24
migrationSteps: Remove com.vaadin.event.LayoutEvents import
Replace LayoutClickListener with individual component click listeners
Use component.addClickListener(e -> ...) for direct click handling
For layout-level clicks, attach click listener to the root Div element
Replace all javax.servlet.* imports with jakarta.servlet.*
Run OpenRewrite ChangePackage recipe: org.openrewrite.java.ChangePackage
Replace all javax.validation.* imports with jakarta.validation.*
Run OpenRewrite ChangePackage recipe: org.openrewrite.java.ChangePackage
Replace all javax.persistence.* imports with jakarta.persistence.*
Run OpenRewrite ChangePackage recipe: org.openrewrite.java.ChangePackage
Verify Spring Data JPA annotations (@Entity, @Table, @Column, @Id) still resolve
Replace all javax.annotation.* imports with jakarta.annotation.*
Specifically: javax.annotation.PostConstruct → jakarta.annotation.PostConstruct
Specifically: javax.annotation.PreDestroy → jakarta.annotation.PreDestroy
Replace all javax.inject.* imports with jakarta.inject.*
Note: Spring Boot uses @Autowired/constructor injection by convention over @Inject
Replace all javax.transaction.* imports with jakarta.transaction.*
Note: Spring @Transactional is preferred over javax/jakarta @Transactional in Spring apps
Replace all javax.ws.rs.* imports with jakarta.ws.rs.*
Note: Consider migrating JAX-RS to Spring MVC @RestController if feasible
Replace all javax.mail.* imports with jakarta.mail.*
Update dependency: javax.mail:mail → jakarta.mail:jakarta.mail-api or org.eclipse.angus:angus-mail
Replace all javax.xml.bind.* imports with jakarta.xml.bind.*
Add dependency: jakarta.xml.bind:jakarta.xml.bind-api and an implementation like glassfish jaxb
Replace all javax.ejb.* imports with jakarta.ejb.*
Consider migrating EJBs to Spring @Service beans for modern Spring Boot architecture

rules.context: Button.ClickEvent is now generic ClickEvent<Button> in Flow
migrationSteps: Replace import: com.vaadin.ui.Button.ClickEvent -> com.vaadin.flow.component.ClickEvent
Update type references: Button.ClickEvent -> ClickEvent<Button>
Flow ClickEvent provides getSource() returning Button, not getButton()
If checking isCtrlKey()/isShiftKey(), use getClickCount() or DOM event details instead

rules.context: Button.ClickListener replaced by lambda-based addClickListener() in Flow
migrationSteps: Remove Button.ClickListener interface implementations entirely
Replace button.addClickListener(this) with button.addClickListener(e -> handleClick(e))
Before: class MyView implements Button.ClickListener { void buttonClick(ClickEvent e) {...} }
After: button.addClickListener(e -> { /* handler code */ })
If the listener is shared across multiple buttons, extract to a method: button.addClickListener(this::handleClick)

rules.context: Wildcard import for Button inner classes - resolve to specific types first
migrationSteps: Resolve wildcard import to specific inner class references (ClickEvent, ClickListener)
Apply COMP-052 for ClickEvent -> ClickEvent<Button>
Apply COMP-053 for ClickListener -> lambda addClickListener()
Remove the wildcard import after resolving all references

rules.context: Window is replaced by Dialog in Flow; CloseEvent becomes DialogCloseActionEvent
migrationSteps: Replace Window with Dialog (see COMP-028)
Window.CloseEvent -> use Dialog addDialogCloseActionHandler or addDetachListener
Before: window.addCloseListener(e -> ...) After: dialog.addDialogCloseActionListener(e -> ...)
Note: DialogCloseActionEvent fires when user tries to close (Esc/overlay click), not after close

rules.context: Window.CloseListener replaced by lambda in Dialog
migrationSteps: Remove Window.CloseListener interface implementation
Replace with dialog.addDialogCloseActionListener(e -> { /* handler */ })
For detecting actual close (not just attempt), use dialog.addDetachListener(e -> ...)
If using windowClose(CloseEvent e), inline the handler as a lambda

rules.context: Wildcard Window inner classes - resolve to CloseEvent, CloseListener, ResizeEvent, ResizeListener
migrationSteps: Resolve to specific types: CloseEvent (COMP-055), CloseListener (COMP-056), ResizeEvent, ResizeListener
Window.ResizeEvent/ResizeListener - use dialog.getElement().addPropertyChangeListener() for size changes or CSS resize
Remove wildcard import after resolving all references

rules.context: TabSheet.Tab inner interface -> standalone Tab component in Flow
migrationSteps: Replace import: com.vaadin.ui.TabSheet.Tab -> com.vaadin.flow.component.tabs.Tab
Flow Tab is a full component, not an inner interface - create with new Tab("label")
Tab properties: setEnabled(), setLabel() exist in Flow; setClosable() -> tab.add(new Button(closeIcon))
Caption/icon setting: tab.setLabel(text) and tab.addComponentAsFirst(icon)

rules.context: TabSheet.SelectedTabChangeEvent -> Tabs.SelectedChangeEvent in Flow
migrationSteps: Replace TabSheet.SelectedTabChangeEvent with Tabs.SelectedChangeEvent
Before: tabSheet.addSelectedTabChangeListener(e -> e.getTabSheet().getSelectedTab())
After: tabs.addSelectedChangeListener(e -> e.getSelectedTab())
Flow event directly provides getSelectedTab() returning the Tab component

rules.context: TabSheet.SelectedTabChangeListener -> lambda on Tabs
migrationSteps: Remove SelectedTabChangeListener interface implementation
Replace with tabs.addSelectedChangeListener(e -> { /* handler */ })
Access selected tab via e.getSelectedTab() instead of casting

rules.context: Wildcard TabSheet inner classes
migrationSteps: Resolve to specific types: Tab (COMP-058), SelectedTabChangeEvent (COMP-059), SelectedTabChangeListener (COMP-060)

Before: implements DropHandler { void drop(DragAndDropEvent e) {...} AcceptCriterion getAcceptCriterion() {...} }
After: DropTarget.create(component).addDropListener(e -> { e.getDragData(); /* handle */ })
Import com.vaadin.flow.component.dnd.DropTarget and DragSource

rules.context: DragAndDropEvent removed; use Flow DnD events
migrationSteps: Replace DragAndDropEvent with specific Flow DnD events
getTransferable() -> use DragStartEvent.getDragData() or DropEvent.getDragData()
getTargetDetails() -> use DropEvent.getComponent() for drop target
Flow DnD: DragSource.create(component), DropTarget.create(component)
Grid: grid.addDragStartListener(), grid.addDropListener()

rules.context: AcceptCriterion removed; use DropEvent validation in Flow
migrationSteps: Remove AcceptCriterion - Flow validates drops in the DropListener
Before: getAcceptCriterion() returning AcceptAll.get() or custom criterion
After: validate in drop listener: dropTarget.addDropListener(e -> { if (isValid(e)) { accept(); } })
For Grid: use grid.setDropMode(GridDropMode.ON_TOP) and filter in listener

rules.context: AcceptAll removed; all drops accepted by default in Flow
migrationSteps: Remove AcceptAll.get() - Flow accepts all drops by default
If using AcceptAll in getAcceptCriterion(), simply remove the method
Drop validation is done in the DropListener if needed

rules.context: Transferable removed; use DragData/DropEvent in Flow
migrationSteps: Remove Transferable interface - Flow uses generic drag data
Replace transferable.getData("itemId") with DragSource.setDragData(myData) and DropEvent.getDragData()
Flow drag data is an Object - cast to your expected type in the drop listener
getSourceComponent() -> DragStartEvent.getComponent() or DropEvent.getDragSourceComponent()

rules.context: Wildcard drag-and-drop - all replaced by Flow DnD API
migrationSteps: Resolve: DropHandler (EVT-025), DragAndDropEvent (EVT-026), DropTarget, Transferable (EVT-029)
All V7 DnD is replaced by com.vaadin.flow.component.dnd package
Key classes: DragSource, DropTarget, DragStartEvent, DragEndEvent, DropEvent
Remove wildcard import and use specific Flow DnD imports

rules.context: Accept criteria package removed; validate in drop listeners
migrationSteps: Remove entire acceptcriteria package usage
AcceptAll -> drops accepted by default (EVT-028)
AcceptCriterion -> validate in drop listener (EVT-027)
ServerSideCriterion -> validate server-side in drop listener
Not/And/Or criteria -> use Java boolean logic in drop listener

rules.context: FontAwesome enum replaced by VaadinIcon or Lumo icons in Flow
migrationSteps: Replace FontAwesome.XXXX with VaadinIcon.XXXX.create() or new Icon(VaadinIcon.XXXX)
Not all FontAwesome icons have VaadinIcon equivalents - check VaadinIcon enum
Common mappings: FontAwesome.EDIT -> VaadinIcon.EDIT, FontAwesome.TRASH -> VaadinIcon.TRASH, FontAwesome.PLUS -> VaadinIcon.PLUS
Import com.vaadin.flow.component.icon.VaadinIcon and com.vaadin.flow.component.icon.Icon

rules.context: Page class moved to Flow; access via UI.getCurrent().getPage()
migrationSteps: Replace import: com.vaadin.server.Page -> com.vaadin.flow.component.page.Page
Page.getCurrent() -> UI.getCurrent().getPage()
page.reload() -> page.reload() (same)
page.setTitle() -> ui.getPage().setTitle()
page.getStyles().add() -> ui.getPage().addStyleSheet() or use @CssImport

rules.context: Page.Styles removed; use @CssImport or page.addStyleSheet()
migrationSteps: Remove Page.Styles usage
For inline styles: use @CssImport annotation on view class
For dynamic styles: ui.getPage().executeJs() to inject style elements
Preferred: create CSS file and use @CssImport("./styles/my-styles.css")

rules.context: Browser resize event - use Page.addBrowserWindowResizeListener in Flow
migrationSteps: Replace com.vaadin.server.Page.BrowserWindowResizeEvent with Flow equivalent
UI.getCurrent().getPage().addBrowserWindowResizeListener(e -> { e.getWidth(); e.getHeight(); })
Import com.vaadin.flow.component.page.BrowserWindowResizeEvent from Flow
Event provides getWidth() and getHeight() - same API

rules.context: BrowserWindowResizeListener -> lambda on Page
migrationSteps: Remove BrowserWindowResizeListener interface implementation
Replace with ui.getPage().addBrowserWindowResizeListener(e -> { /* handler */ })
Lambda receives BrowserWindowResizeEvent with getWidth()/getHeight()

rules.context: Wildcard server import - resolve to specific classes first
migrationSteps: Resolve wildcard to specific server classes used in code
Page -> com.vaadin.flow.component.page.Page (SRV-017)
FontAwesome -> VaadinIcon (SRV-016)
VaadinSession -> com.vaadin.flow.server.VaadinSession
Resource/ThemeResource -> see SRV-022, SRV-023
Remove wildcard import after resolving

rules.context: Resource interface -> StreamResource in Flow; external resources use standard URLs
migrationSteps: Replace com.vaadin.server.Resource with com.vaadin.flow.server.StreamResource for dynamic content
For external URLs: use String URL directly (no wrapper class needed)
StreamResource: new StreamResource("file.pdf", () -> new ByteArrayInputStream(data))
For images: use Image component with setSrc(streamResource) or setSrc("url")

rules.context: ThemeResource removed; use CSS-based theming or static resources in Flow
migrationSteps: Remove ThemeResource entirely - themes work differently in Flow
For images: place in src/main/resources/META-INF/resources/images/ and reference as "/images/file.png"
For icons: use VaadinIcon, LumoIcon, or Icon class with SVG
For CSS backgrounds: use @CssImport and reference static resources in CSS
Theme customization: use @Theme annotation with Lumo theme variants

rules.context: FileDownloader removed; use Anchor with StreamResource in Flow
migrationSteps: Remove FileDownloader extension pattern
Before: FileDownloader fd = new FileDownloader(resource); fd.extend(button);
After: Anchor anchor = new Anchor(streamResource, "Download"); anchor.getElement().setAttribute("download", true);
For button-triggered download: wrap anchor around button, or use page.open() with StreamResource
Import com.vaadin.flow.component.html.Anchor and com.vaadin.flow.server.StreamResource

rules.context: BrowserWindowOpener removed; use Page.open() or Anchor target=_blank
migrationSteps: Remove BrowserWindowOpener extension pattern
Before: new BrowserWindowOpener(url).extend(button)
After (link): new Anchor(url, "Open").setTarget("_blank")
After (button click): button.addClickListener(e -> UI.getCurrent().getPage().open(url))

rules.context: WebBrowser moved to Flow server package
migrationSteps: Replace import: com.vaadin.server.WebBrowser -> com.vaadin.flow.server.WebBrowser
Access via VaadinSession.getCurrent().getBrowser()
isChrome(), isFirefox(), isSafari(), etc. available in Flow

rules.context: VaadinSession.State enum exists in Flow but check for API changes
migrationSteps: VaadinSession is available in Flow: com.vaadin.flow.server.VaadinSession
State enum (OPEN, CLOSING, CLOSED) is similar in Flow
Access: VaadinSession.getCurrent().getState()
Session lifecycle mostly unchanged; session attributes work the same

rules.context: StreamResource.StreamSource -> InputStreamFactory functional interface in Flow
migrationSteps: Remove StreamSource interface implementation
Before: new StreamResource.StreamSource() { InputStream getStream() { return ...; } }
After: new StreamResource("file.pdf", () -> inputStream) - uses InputStreamFactory lambda
StreamResource constructor in Flow: new StreamResource(filename, InputStreamFactory)
Import com.vaadin.flow.server.StreamResource

rules.context: StreamVariable removed; use Upload component with Receiver in Flow
migrationSteps: Remove StreamVariable interface - used for streaming uploads in V7
Replace with Upload component: Upload upload = new Upload(); upload.setReceiver(...)
For progress: upload.addProgressListener(e -> e.getReadBytes())
For completion: upload.addSucceededListener(e -> e.getFileName())

rules.context: Responsive extension removed; use CSS media queries or FormLayout responsive steps
migrationSteps: Remove Responsive.makeResponsive(component) - not needed in Flow
Flow components are responsive by default (web components with Shadow DOM)
For responsive layouts: use FormLayout with setResponsiveSteps()
For custom breakpoints: use CSS @media queries in @CssImport files

rules.context: VaadinServletService moved to Flow server package
migrationSteps: Replace import: com.vaadin.server.VaadinServletService -> com.vaadin.flow.server.VaadinServletService
Most API methods are similar in Flow
Access current service: VaadinService.getCurrent()

rules.context: UserError removed; use component.setErrorMessage() or Binder validation
migrationSteps: Remove UserError - Flow components have built-in error display
Before: field.setComponentError(new UserError("Invalid value"))
After: field.setErrorMessage("Invalid value"); field.setInvalid(true);
For form validation: use Binder with validators, errors display automatically
To clear: field.setInvalid(false)

rules.context: DefaultErrorHandler -> ErrorHandler functional interface in Flow
migrationSteps: Replace extends DefaultErrorHandler with ErrorHandler implementation
Before: VaadinSession.getCurrent().setErrorHandler(new DefaultErrorHandler() { void error(ErrorEvent e) {...} })
After: VaadinSession.getCurrent().setErrorHandler(event -> { /* handle event.getThrowable() */ })
Import com.vaadin.flow.server.ErrorHandler and com.vaadin.flow.server.ErrorEvent

rules.context: BootstrapListener removed; use IndexHtmlRequestListener in Flow
migrationSteps: Replace BootstrapListener with com.vaadin.flow.server.communication.IndexHtmlRequestListener
Register as a VaadinServiceInitListener in META-INF/services/
Before: modifyBootstrapPage(BootstrapPageResponse response)
After: modifyIndexHtmlResponse(IndexHtmlResponse response)

rules.context: SessionInitListener moved to Flow server package
migrationSteps: Replace import: com.vaadin.server.SessionInitListener -> com.vaadin.flow.server.SessionInitListener
Register via VaadinServiceInitListener: service.addSessionInitListener(e -> ...)
Create META-INF/services/com.vaadin.flow.server.VaadinServiceInitListener file

rules.context: DownloadStream removed; use StreamResource with Anchor in Flow
migrationSteps: Remove DownloadStream entirely
Replace with StreamResource: new StreamResource("file.pdf", () -> inputStream)
Attach to Anchor with download attribute
Content-Type is inferred or set via streamResource.setContentType()

rules.context: @SpringUI removed; Spring Boot auto-configures Vaadin Flow
migrationSteps: Remove @SpringUI annotation entirely - not needed in Flow
Flow with Spring Boot auto-discovers @Route views and manages the UI lifecycle
The UI class concept is replaced by @Route views + AppShellConfigurator
Before: @SpringUI @Theme("mytheme") public class MyUI extends UI { void init(VaadinRequest) {...} }
After: @Route("") public class MainView extends VerticalLayout { public MainView() { /* init */ } }

rules.context: @EnableVaadin removed; Spring Boot starter auto-configures
migrationSteps: Remove @EnableVaadin annotation - vaadin-spring-boot-starter auto-configures everything
Ensure vaadin-spring-boot-starter dependency is in build.gradle/pom.xml
No manual configuration needed for component scanning in Flow + Spring Boot

rules.context: SpringVaadinServlet removed; auto-configured by Spring Boot
migrationSteps: Remove SpringVaadinServlet class/configuration - Spring Boot auto-configures the servlet
If custom servlet configuration needed: extend SpringServlet from com.vaadin.flow.spring
Custom init params: use application.properties with vaadin.* prefix instead

rules.context: CurrentInstance still available but rarely needed in Flow
migrationSteps: CurrentInstance utility exists in Flow but usage patterns change
For UI access: UI.getCurrent() (same pattern)
For VaadinSession: VaadinSession.getCurrent()
If using for custom ThreadLocal storage: consider Spring @RequestScope or @SessionScope beans instead

rules.context: Sizeable.Unit removed; use CSS strings for sizing in Flow
migrationSteps: Remove Sizeable.Unit - Flow uses CSS string values for sizing
Before: component.setWidth(100, Unit.PERCENTAGE) After: component.setWidth("100%")
Before: component.setHeight(200, Unit.PIXELS) After: component.setHeight("200px")
setSizeFull(), setWidthFull(), setHeightFull() work the same in Flow

rules.context: CustomizedSystemMessages moved to Flow server package
migrationSteps: Replace import: com.vaadin.server.CustomizedSystemMessages -> com.vaadin.flow.server.CustomizedSystemMessages
Register via SystemMessagesProvider in VaadinServiceInitListener
API is similar: setSessionExpiredCaption(), setCommunicationErrorMessage(), etc.

rules.context: SystemMessagesProvider moved to Flow server package
migrationSteps: Replace import: com.vaadin.server.SystemMessagesProvider -> com.vaadin.flow.server.SystemMessagesProvider
Register in VaadinServiceInitListener: service.setSystemMessagesProvider(info -> { ... return msgs; })

rules.context: VaadinServletRequest moved to Flow server package
migrationSteps: Replace import: com.vaadin.server.VaadinServletRequest -> com.vaadin.flow.server.VaadinServletRequest
Access: VaadinServletRequest.getCurrent() or cast from VaadinRequest

rules.context: VaadinServletResponse moved to Flow server package
migrationSteps: Replace import: com.vaadin.server.VaadinServletResponse -> com.vaadin.flow.server.VaadinServletResponse
Access: VaadinServletResponse.getCurrent() or cast from VaadinResponse

rules.context: ViewChangeEvent -> BeforeEnterEvent/BeforeLeaveEvent in Flow
migrationSteps: Replace ViewChangeEvent with BeforeEnterEvent or BeforeLeaveEvent depending on usage
event.getViewName() -> the @Route value of the target view
event.getParameters() -> use HasUrlParameter<T> interface on the view class
event.getOldView() -> use BeforeLeaveEvent in the departing view
event.getNewView() -> use BeforeEnterEvent in the arriving view

rules.context: ViewChangeListener -> BeforeEnterObserver/BeforeLeaveObserver interfaces in Flow
migrationSteps: Remove ViewChangeListener interface
For entering a view: implement BeforeEnterObserver on the view class
For leaving a view: implement BeforeLeaveObserver on the view class
beforeEnter(BeforeEnterEvent) replaces beforeViewChange(ViewChangeEvent) for navigation guard
beforeLeave(BeforeLeaveEvent) with event.postpone() for unsaved changes confirmation

rules.context: Navigator removed; use @Route and UI.navigate() in Flow
migrationSteps: Remove Navigator entirely - Flow uses automatic routing via @Route annotations
navigator.navigateTo("view") -> UI.getCurrent().navigate("view")
navigator.addView("name", ViewClass.class) -> not needed, use @Route("name") on view class
navigator.addViewChangeListener() -> implement BeforeEnterObserver/BeforeLeaveObserver on views

rules.context: ViewDisplay removed; use RouterLayout in Flow
migrationSteps: Remove ViewDisplay interface - Flow uses RouterLayout for parent layouts
Before: implements ViewDisplay { void showView(View view) { setContent(view); } }
After: @Route(value = "child", layout = MainLayout.class) on child views
MainLayout implements RouterLayout
Import com.vaadin.flow.router.RouterLayout

rules.context: ViewProvider removed; Flow auto-discovers @Route annotated views
migrationSteps: Remove ViewProvider implementations - not needed in Flow
Flow automatically discovers all classes annotated with @Route at startup
For dynamic routing: use HasUrlParameter<T> on view classes
For access control: implement BeforeEnterObserver and use event.rerouteToError() or event.forwardTo()

rules.context: Wildcard navigator import - resolve to specific classes
migrationSteps: Resolve to specific types: View (NAV-001), Navigator (NAV-008), ViewChangeListener (NAV-007), ViewDisplay (NAV-009), ViewProvider (NAV-010)
All navigation in Flow uses @Route annotations + UI.navigate()
Remove wildcard import after resolving

rules.context: Runo theme removed; use Lumo theme in Flow
migrationSteps: Remove all Runo theme constant references (Runo.BUTTON_SMALL, Runo.PANEL_LIGHT, etc.)
Flow uses Lumo theme by default - no explicit theme constants needed
Runo.BUTTON_SMALL -> button.addThemeVariants(ButtonVariant.LUMO_SMALL)
Runo.BUTTON_DEFAULT -> button.addThemeVariants(ButtonVariant.LUMO_PRIMARY)
Runo.PANEL_LIGHT -> use CSS custom properties: --lumo-contrast-5pct for light backgrounds
For custom styling: use @CssImport and Lumo CSS custom properties (--lumo-*)

rules.context: @Theme annotation moved to Flow with different usage
migrationSteps: Replace import: com.vaadin.annotations.Theme -> com.vaadin.flow.theme.Theme
Before: @Theme("runo") on UI class
After: @Theme("my-theme") on a class implementing AppShellConfigurator, or omit for default Lumo
Lumo is the default theme - @Theme annotation only needed for custom themes

rules.context: @StyleSheet replaced by @CssImport in Flow
migrationSteps: Replace @StyleSheet("mystyles.css") with @CssImport("./styles/mystyles.css")
Place CSS files in frontend/styles/ directory (resolved from src/main/frontend/)
For external stylesheets: @StyleSheet("https://...") still works in Flow but @CssImport is preferred
Import com.vaadin.flow.component.dependency.CssImport

rules.context: @JavaScript replaced by @JsModule in Flow
migrationSteps: Replace @JavaScript("myscript.js") with @JsModule("./myscript.js")
Place JS files in src/main/frontend/ directory
For external scripts: use page.addJavaScript("https://...") or @JavaScript for URLs
@JsModule is for ES module imports; @JavaScript for non-module scripts

rules.context: @PreserveOnRefresh moved to Flow router package
migrationSteps: Replace import: com.vaadin.annotations.PreserveOnRefresh -> com.vaadin.flow.router.PreserveOnRefresh
Apply @PreserveOnRefresh on @Route view classes instead of UI class
Behavior is the same: preserves view state on browser refresh

rules.context: @Title replaced by @PageTitle in Flow
migrationSteps: Replace @Title("My Page") with @PageTitle("My Page")
Import com.vaadin.flow.router.PageTitle
Apply on @Route view classes; for dynamic titles implement HasDynamicTitle

rules.context: @Push annotation moved to Flow
migrationSteps: Replace import: com.vaadin.annotations.Push -> com.vaadin.flow.component.page.Push
Apply on class implementing AppShellConfigurator (not on UI class)
@Push enables server push (WebSocket by default)

rules.context: Wildcard annotations import - resolve to specific annotations
migrationSteps: Resolve to specific annotations: Theme (THM-002), StyleSheet (THM-003), JavaScript (THM-004), PreserveOnRefresh (THM-005), Title (THM-006), Push (THM-007)
All annotations moved to different Flow packages
Remove wildcard import after resolving

rules.context: ContentMode enum removed; use Html component or element API in Flow
migrationSteps: Remove ContentMode import entirely
ContentMode.HTML -> use Html component: new Html("rich text")
ContentMode.TEXT -> use Span or Text component (default behavior)
ContentMode.PREFORMATTED -> use Pre component or Span with white-space: pre CSS
Before: new Label("bold", ContentMode.HTML) After: new Html("bold")
IMPORTANT: Html component requires a single root element - wrap in <span> if needed

rules.context: ButtonState removed; no shared state model in Flow
migrationSteps: Remove ButtonState entirely - shared state model does not exist in Flow
Button properties are accessed via component API: button.setText(), button.setEnabled(), etc.
If extending ButtonState in custom connector: rewrite as a Flow web component
Server-side state is managed through element properties: getElement().setProperty("key", value)

rules.context: MouseButton enum removed; use ClickEvent.getButton() in Flow
migrationSteps: Remove MouseEventDetails.MouseButton import
Flow ClickEvent provides getButton() returning int (0=left, 1=middle, 2=right)
Before: if (details.getButton() == MouseButton.LEFT)
After: if (event.getButton() == 0) // left click
For context menu: use ContextMenu component

rules.context: MultiSelectMode removed; Grid uses SelectionMode.MULTI in Flow
migrationSteps: Remove MultiSelectMode import
Replace with Grid.setSelectionMode(Grid.SelectionMode.MULTI)
MultiSelectMode.DEFAULT -> Grid.SelectionMode.MULTI (click to select)
Access selection: grid.asMultiSelect().addValueChangeListener(e -> e.getValue())

rules.context: Resolution enum removed; use DatePicker/DateTimePicker with step in Flow
migrationSteps: Remove Resolution import - Flow DatePicker uses LocalDate (day resolution always)
Resolution.DAY -> DatePicker (default)
Resolution.HOUR/MINUTE/SECOND -> DateTimePicker with step: dateTimePicker.setStep(Duration.ofMinutes(15))
Resolution.MONTH/YEAR -> no direct equivalent; use Select or custom picker

rules.context: MarginInfo removed; use CSS or ThemableLayout.setMargin() in Flow
migrationSteps: Remove MarginInfo - Flow layouts use simpler margin API
Before: layout.setMargin(new MarginInfo(true, false, true, false))
After: layout.getStyle().set("margin-top", "var(--lumo-space-m)").set("margin-bottom", "var(--lumo-space-m)")
Or use layout.setMargin(true) for uniform margin on all sides

rules.context: WindowMode removed; Dialog does not have normal/maximized modes by default
migrationSteps: Remove WindowMode import - Dialog replaces Window in Flow
WindowMode.NORMAL -> default Dialog state
WindowMode.MAXIMIZED -> dialog.setSizeFull()
For toggle maximize: add a maximize button that toggles between setSizeFull() and default size

rules.context: FilteringMode removed; ComboBox uses setItems with filter in Flow
migrationSteps: Remove FilteringMode import
FilteringMode.CONTAINS -> default ComboBox behavior in Flow
FilteringMode.STARTSWITH -> comboBox.setFilter((item, query) -> item.startsWith(query))
FilteringMode.OFF -> comboBox.setItems(items) without filter callback

rules.context: BorderStyle removed; use CSS styling in Flow
migrationSteps: Remove BorderStyle import - purely CSS concern in Flow
component.getStyle().set("border", "1px solid var(--lumo-contrast-20pct)")
Or use addClassName() with CSS class defining border styles

rules.context: VerticalDropLocation -> GridDropLocation for Grid DnD in Flow
migrationSteps: Replace VerticalDropLocation with GridDropLocation for Grid drag-and-drop
TOP -> GridDropLocation.ABOVE
MIDDLE -> GridDropLocation.ON_TOP
BOTTOM -> GridDropLocation.BELOW
Import com.vaadin.flow.component.grid.dnd.GridDropLocation

rules.context: Wildcard shared.ui - all shared state removed in Flow
migrationSteps: Resolve to specific types: ContentMode (THM-009), MultiSelectMode (THM-012), Resolution (THM-013), etc.
All com.vaadin.shared.* classes are removed in Flow - the shared-state architecture is gone
Replace with component API calls or CSS styling
Remove wildcard import after resolving

rules.context: ServerRpc removed; use @ClientCallable methods in Flow
migrationSteps: Remove ServerRpc interface - RPC model replaced by element API in Flow
Before: interface MyRpc extends ServerRpc { void onClick(MouseEventDetails details); }
After: @ClientCallable public void onClick(String data) { /* handler */ }
Client calls server via element.$server.onClick(data) in JavaScript

rules.context: ClientRpc removed; use Element.executeJs() in Flow
migrationSteps: Remove ClientRpc interface - replaced by element.executeJs() in Flow
Before: getRpcProxy(MyClientRpc.class).showMessage("text")
After: getElement().executeJs("this.showMessage($0)", "text")
For component state updates: getElement().setProperty("key", value)

rules.context: @Connect removed; Flow uses web components, not GWT connectors
migrationSteps: Remove @Connect annotation - GWT connector model does not exist in Flow
Flow web components use @Tag, @NpmPackage, and @JsModule instead
Before: @Connect(MyServerComponent.class) public class MyConnector extends AbstractComponentConnector
After: @Tag("my-component") @JsModule("./my-component.js") public class MyComponent extends Component
Full rewrite required: create a Lit/web component JS file and a Java wrapper class

rules.context: Color class removed; use CSS color strings in Flow
migrationSteps: Remove com.vaadin.shared.ui.colorpicker.Color
Use CSS color strings directly: "#FF0000", "rgb(255,0,0)", "var(--lumo-primary-color)"
component.getStyle().set("color", "#FF0000")

rules.context: GWT connectors removed; rewrite as Flow web component wrapper
migrationSteps: Remove AbstractComponentConnector entirely - GWT client-side framework removed in Flow
Flow uses web components (Lit/Polymer) instead of GWT widgets
Create JS web component: class MyComponent extends LitElement { ... }
Create Java wrapper: @Tag("my-component") @JsModule("./my-component.js") public class MyComponent extends Component
State sync via getElement().setProperty()/getElement().addPropertyChangeListener()

rules.context: Entire client (GWT) package removed; rewrite as web components
migrationSteps: All com.vaadin.client.* classes are GWT-based and completely removed in Flow
Connectors -> @Tag + @JsModule web component wrappers
Widgets (VButton, VLabel, etc.) -> Flow uses native web components
StateChangeEvent -> use element property change listeners
This requires a full architectural rewrite from GWT to web components

rules.context: GWT widget classes removed; use Flow web components
migrationSteps: All VButton, VLabel, VVerticalLayout etc. are GWT widgets - removed in Flow
Flow renders via web components in the browser, not GWT widgets
For custom widgets: create a Lit element JS file and Java wrapper
Remove all com.vaadin.client.ui.* imports

rules.context: Wildcard UI import - must resolve to specific component imports first
migrationSteps: CRITICAL: Resolve this wildcard to the specific com.vaadin.ui classes actually used in the file
Search for all V7 type references in the file: Button, TextField, Label, Grid, Table, etc.
Apply the corresponding COMP-xxx rule for each resolved type
Flow components live in different sub-packages: com.vaadin.flow.component.button.Button, com.vaadin.flow.component.textfield.TextField, etc.
Do NOT replace with com.vaadin.flow.component.* - use explicit imports
